summary:
The primary cause of low birth weight is premature delivery, which constitutes roughly 70% of such cases globally according to World Health Organization estimates. This occurs because early birth limits time available for fetal growth even when other risks are absent. \boxed{B}

The correct choice based on this analysis should reflect "premature delivery" as the leading reason behind low birth weight across populations.\boxed{B}

This occurs because early birth limits time available for fetal growth even when other risks are absent. \boxed{B}

The correct choice based on this analysis should reflect "premature delivery" as the leading reason behind low birth weight across populations.\boxed{B}. Answer choice: A
